actions/setup-node#529.body:
**Description:**
Similar to how we can set the node version I'd like to set the npm version. 
```
node-version: 1.16.x
npm-version: 8.5.5
```

There is a similar issue here https://github.com/actions/setup-node/issues/213, but it's for a different reason and this one can be resolved by setting the node version. However this is not enough for the problems I have been facing.

**Justification:**
Currently I am struggling with a bug in npm from versions 8.11 to 8.13. These are automatically installed when you chose node version 1.16. This means I have to set back my npm version so the ci can run, which I can do in an extra ci step.

However to me the issue is that the npm version will change over time, without our input. We'd like control over this to prevent issues like the one above from showing up.

**Are you willing to submit a PR?**
Not currently but I might in the future.

aws/aws-sdk-js-v3#7379.body:
### Checkboxes for prior research

- [x] I've gone through [Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-javascript/v3/developer-guide) and [API reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSJavaScriptSDK/v3/latest)
- [x] I've checked [AWS Forums](https://forums.aws.amazon.com) and [StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/aws-sdk-js).
- [x] I've searched for [previous similar issues](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3/issues) and didn't find any solution.

### Describe the bug

It seems like the `partSize` option in the `Upload` class constructor is silently ignored due to an internal computation which results in the default part size of 5 MB.

To reproduce this, I was trying to upload a ~6.5 GB file and pass in a `partSize` of 50 or 100 MB (`50 * 1024 * 1024` or `100 * 1024 * 1024`), and found that the parts were always 5 MB no matter what I specified.

After inspecting the internals of the `Upload` object instance, it does seem like the `partSize` was 5 MB, and the one I specified was not used. Looking at the code, I think this line is the reason:

https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3/blob/ad1514df905b7b661f7f81050f5f2102d31e5cfa/lib/lib-storage/src/Upload.ts#L96

When I pass in a `Body` stream that is 7019552768 bytes (~6.5 GB), it assigns `this.partSize` to the result `Math.max(Upload.MIN_PART_SIZE = 5 * 1024 * 1024 = 5 MB, 7019552768 / 10000 = 701955.2768 = 0.66 MB)`, which of course takes the 5 MB over the 0.66 MB. It doesn't look at `options.partSize` at all.

### Regression Issue

- [ ] Select this option if this issue appears to be a regression.

### SDK version number

```
@aws-sdk/client-s3@3.896.0
@aws-sdk/lib-storage@3.896.0
```

### Which JavaScript Runtime is this issue in?

Node.js

### Details of the browser/Node.js/ReactNative version

v24.8.0

### Reproduction Steps

Try to create an upload using the `Upload` class and specify a custom `partSize` that is larger than 5 MB.

### Observed Behavior

`options.partSize` is ignored

### Expected Behavior

`options.partSize` is respected, assuming it is [within the minimum and maximum values (5 MB -> 5 GiB)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/qfacts.html)

### Possible Solution

I am able to workaround this issue by overriding the `MIN_PART_SIZE` property, but it's not ideal:

```ts
/* @ts-ignore */
Upload.MIN_PART_SIZE = PART_SIZE;
```

### Additional Information/Context

_No response_
aws/aws-sdk-js-v3#7379.title: Upload class from @aws-sdk/lib-storage ignores `partSize` option

docker-hardened-images/catalog#92.body:
### Docker Hardened Image

nginx

### Bug Description

The [DHI Nginx Guide](https://hub.docker.com/hardened-images/catalog/dhi/nginx/guides) includes a section titled "Using environment variables in Nginx configuration" which describes the `envsubst` templating feature:

> Out-of-the-box, Nginx doesn't support environment variables inside most configuration blocks. But this image has a function, which will extract environment variables before Nginx starts.

This documentation appears to be copied from the [standard nginx Docker image README](https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx), which does support this feature via its `docker-entrypoint.sh` script.

However, the hardened image does not include this functionality. Inspecting the [image specifications](https://hub.docker.com/hardened-images/catalog/dhi/nginx/images/nginx%2Fdebian-12%2Fmainline/sha256-b5c2875ce526ed9bc1be63ee3983296fe8e82904ca1e29bfdbd64e77954c0aa4/specifications) shows:

```yaml
entrypoint:
    - nginx
cmd:
    - -g
    - daemon off;
```

The image runs `nginx` directly without the entrypoint script that processes templates in `/etc/nginx/templates/*.template` using envsubst.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a template file expecting envsubst processing:
   ```
   # /etc/nginx/templates/default.conf.template
   server {
       listen ${NGINX_PORT};
       ...
   }
   ```

2. Run the container with the environment variable:
   ```bash
   docker run -e NGINX_PORT=8080 dhi.io/nginx:1
   ```


### Expected Behavior

Either:
1. The envsubst templating feature should be enabled in the hardened image (add the entrypoint script or equivalent functionality), OR
2. The "Using environment variables in Nginx configuration" section should be removed from the DHI nginx documentation to avoid confusion

### Actual Behavior

Template is ignored, nginx uses default config (or fails if no default exists)

### Environment

- Image: `dhi.io/nginx:1` (1.29.4-debian12)
- Platforms: linux/amd64, linux/arm64

### Relevant Logs

```shell

```

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this bug hasn't been reported before
- [x] I have provided all the requested information above
- [x] I have tested this with the latest available version of the hardened image
docker-hardened-images/catalog#92.title: [bug]: nginx docs claim envsubst templating support, but feature is not available

mantinedev/mantine#3330.body: ### What package has an issue @mantine/core ### Describe the bug 1. Create a Modal **with transition** that uses `transform` under the hood. Let's say, `scale`. 2. Put `SegmentedControl` component inside of this modal. 3. Open the modal and observe that active value height (and sometimes width) is calculated incorrectly. The result depends on the selected transition in the first step so sometimes both height and width are 0 and sometimes width is correct but height is a bit larger than expected. ### What version of @mantine/hooks page do you have in package.json? 5.10.0 ### If possible, please include a link to a codesandbox with the reproduced problem https://codesandbox.io/s/reverent-jones-dq0icp?file=/src/App.tsx ### Do you know how to fix the issue None ### Are you willing to participate in fixing this issue and create a pull request with the fix None ### Possible fix I believe the possible fix might be to detect any parent transition, wait till the `transitionend` (or `animationend` if necessary) and recalculate the size one more time. I haven't really gone deep into the code so I'm not sure if this solution would be appropriate but from what I'm aware of it should kind of be. Another thing to consider here: a user may want to implement some custom transitions around the modal or any parent container that contains `SegmentedControl` in there, so this should not only work with built-in `Transition` component. Another fix is to change the way size is calculated but this one requires more investigation. ### Related Issues #585 #2994

medplum/medplum#3803.body:
Currently, calling `MedplumClient#clear` is quite destructive and blows away **all** local storage entries regardless of if they're Medplum-managed.

https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/1ac475829270460fdcc008e60a30e93c2d62d2ad/packages/core/src/client.ts#L805-L814

This is a side-effect of logging out:
https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/1ac475829270460fdcc008e60a30e93c2d62d2ad/packages/core/src/client.ts#L1116-L1120

I'd like to propose introducing a prefix to all local storage keys that are created by Medplum to enable `#clear` to be more surgical and only remove Medplum created/managed local storage entries. Additionally, this would reduce the likelihood of key clashing with other systems/extensions that leverage local storage.
i.e. `@medplum:${key}`
`@medplum:logins`
`@medplum:activeLogin`

This would be a breaking change if it became the default behavior and FWIW, the `MedplumClient` does already expose storage as an optional input so clients could specify their own local storage wrapper that applies key prefixes and a more surgical removal.
medplum/medplum#3803.title: Namespace Medplum-managed local storage keys

medplum/medplum#4497.body:
The Agent logs are currently stored within the application directory. As these logs roll over after 2MB, they can clutter this directory. It has been requested to allow for the log (current and archived) to go to a specific location on the host. This destination could be another folder inside the application directory (which is not removed if the Agent is removed), a different absolute directory on the same host environment or a network share (using a Windows mapped drive). This should be either supportable during the installer process (maybe as a selective checkbox that launches a screen after clicking Next?), allowing to select a location or configurable post-installation via the agent.properties. Not selecting a different location (or even showing that screen?) should use the default application installation directory for the logs.

Any upgrade to a current Agent installation, should only allow for a change to the agent.config and not prompt for a different destination.
medplum/medplum#4497.title: Agent allow for configurable log directory

medplum/medplum#5126.body:
Currently we have a generic on the type of the `input` for `BotEvent` that does not indicate to users that it's possible for the bot to receive an input which is `undefined` in the case that a user executing a bot does not provide a body to the request.

In general, we probably should also remove the generic because it's not possible to really know at runtime what input you could receive on a given bot. Our examples do not provide any indication that this is the case and neither do our types, so this is probably something that could come back to bite Medplum users later, especially in the cases of their own users fuzz testing inputs.

The recommendation:
1. Add `undefined` to the type union
2. Remove generic (or only add it to the union itself instead of overriding it), put the union directly on the `input` field of `BotEvent`
medplum/medplum#5126.title: Fix types for `BotEvent`

medplum/medplum#5787.body:
Is it possible to put the Task resource into the Patient compartment in Medplum?

In FHIR R4, the Task resource is not in the Patient compartment. This prevents the accounts/compartments of a linked Patient resource from propagating to a newly created Task resource. In systems that leverage account/compartment propagation such that access policies auto-magically include new resources, the absence of Tasks in the Patient compartment is a pretty nasty sharp edge.

One fairly compelling reason to deviate from the spec here is that in R5, Task was added to the Patient compartment. Medplum has previously back-ported R5 functionality to R4 where changes are compatible and improve/enhance the product. IMHO at least, this would be a great improvement as we started using the Task resource and realized late into development that accounts and compartments were not propagating as one would expect, given how other resources work.
medplum/medplum#5787.title: Add the Task resource to the Patient compartment

## Description

I am encountering an issue when trying to filter `Group` resources based on the `EvidenceVariable` referenced in `Group.characteristic[n].valueReference`. This functionality is valuable for scenarios where groups need to be searched or listed based on their characteristics defined by `EvidenceVariable`. However, the current Medplum and FHIR search capabilities do not appear to support this use case due to the following limitations:

- The `value` search parameter does not seem to support references to `EvidenceVariable` resources.
- The `characteristic-value` search parameter is unsupported due to its `composite` type.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/282f8e23-f641-4ab6-bc95-f30d68a14eb6)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8bfe49f-2222-44d2-bf9b-5836a21b1f83)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Attempt to search for `Group` resources using a filter on `Group.characteristic[n].valueReference`.
2. Observe that the `value` search parameter does not support `Reference` data types.
3. Note that the `characteristic-value` parameter is unsupported and cannot be used.

## Related Resources

- [Medplum API Documentation: Group Resource](https://www.medplum.com/docs/api/fhir/resources/group)
- [Example Group](https://app.medplum.com/Group/01946aab-c424-76a1-985b-353ddc0eaa92/details)

  ```json
  {
    "resourceType": "Group",
    "type": "person",
    "actual": false,
    "active": true,
    "name": "NCT00677521_Comparison_Group_1",
    "characteristic": [
      {
        "code": {
          "text": "Exposed to"
        },
        "valueReference": {
          "reference": "EvidenceVariable/<uuid>",
          "type": "EvidenceVariable",
          "display": "Drug: Acarbose"
        },
        "exclude": false
      }
    ],
  }
  ```

medplum/medplum#6302.body:
Yesterday we reverted `validator` because it started more strictly validating UUID string, especially the "version" byte.

It turns out some customers migrated legacy data from systems that used UUID strings that did not follow the exact technical specifications, and did not correctly use the "version" byte, and then started failing the validator.

We reverted `validator`: https://github.com/medplum/medplum/pull/6289

We should also add a test somewhere in `server` to avoid this from happening again.  At this point, I think we want to validate that UUIDs are UUID-ish.

The FHIR spec uses this regex: https://hl7.org/FHIR/datatypes.html#uuid

```
urn:uuid:[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}
```
medplum/medplum#6302.title: Add tests for legacy UUIDs

The IUA specification describes how a FHIR server can accept an authorization token from an external auth provider, _on every resource request_

However, the docs are a bit unclear. This might be completely equivalent to [OAuth RFC7662](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7662)

One major design question is how this would work with Medplum's Project setup, and whether this is possible

medplum/medplum#6483.body:
# Implement Unauthenticated Webhooks with Signature Validation

## Overview
This PR implements support for unauthenticated webhook endpoints that can trigger Bot executions. 
The implementation requires signature headers for security and is designed to support integration 
with third-party services like Cal.com, eFax, Twilio, and SendGrid.

## Implementation Details
- Created a new endpoint: `/webhook/{ProjectMembership.id}` that accepts both GET and POST requests
- Enforced signature header presence (X-Signature, X-HMAC-Signature, X-Cal-Signature-256, X-Twilio-Email-Event-Webhook-Signature)
- Added validation to ensure the ProjectMembership references a Bot resource
- Implemented simplified responses (status codes only) appropriate for webhook consumers
- Added comprehensive tests for the new functionality

## Design Decisions
- We've opted to check for signature header presence rather than implementing verification logic because:
  1. Each service implements HMAC signatures differently (key formats, hashing algorithms, payload preparation)
  2. A general-purpose verification solution would add significant complexity without guaranteeing compatibility
  3. Users can implement service-specific signature verification in their Bot code if needed
  4. This approach maintains flexibility while providing a basic security check

## Use Cases
This implementation enables Medplum to support several important integration scenarios:
- Calendar scheduling via Cal.com
- Fax document handling via eFax
- SMS messaging via Twilio
- Email processing via SendGrid

## Security Considerations
- All webhook requests must include a valid signature header
- The webhook is tied to a specific ProjectMembership ID rather than exposing Bot IDs directly
- Responses are minimized to avoid leaking sensitive information
- Service-specific signature verification can be implemented in Bot code as needed

## Testing
Added tests that verify:
- Signature header validation
- ProjectMembership existence and type verification
- Successful webhook execution with various content types

Closes #6335
medplum/medplum#6483.title: Fixes #6335 - unauthenticated webhooks

medplum/medplum#6603.body:
## Background

The `@medplum/bot-layer` package serves as a reference for the AWS Lambda Layer used by Medplum Bots. Currently, we have inconsistency in how dependencies are declared:

* Some packages like `@medplum/core` are in both `dependencies` and `peerDependencies`
* Others like `@medplum/ccda` are only in `peerDependencies`

This inconsistency may cause confusion about which approach is correct, particularly given how npm's handling of peer dependencies has changed over time.

## Issue

We need to standardize our approach to dependency management in the `@medplum/bot-layer` package to ensure:

1. Consistent developer experience
2. Clear documentation of which packages are available in the Lambda Layer
3. Proper functionality with modern npm (7+)

## Current package.json

```js
{
  "name": "@medplum/bot-layer",
  "version": "4.1.4",
  "description": "Medplum Bot Lambda Layer",
  // ...trimmed for brevity...
  "dependencies": {
    "@medplum/core": "4.1.4",
    "@medplum/definitions": "4.1.4",
    "fast-xml-parser": "5.2.3",
    // ...other dependencies...
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "@medplum/ccda": "4.1.4",
    "@medplum/core": "4.1.4",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.0.9",
    // ...other peer dependencies...
  }
}
```

## Proposal

**Standardize by including all packages in both `dependencies` and `peerDependencies`**:

1. Add `@medplum/ccda` (and any other packages only in `peerDependencies`) to `dependencies` with exact versions
2. Keep all entries in `peerDependencies` with semver flexibility (^ prefix)

## Rationale

While this approach creates some redundancy with npm 7+, it's optimal for our specific use case:

### Why both dependencies AND peerDependencies?

1. **Understanding npm's behavior evolution**:
   - npm 1-2: Automatically installed peer dependencies
   - npm 3-6: Only showed warnings for missing peer dependencies
   - npm 7+: Restored auto-installation of peer dependencies

2. **Dependencies benefits**:
   - Ensures exact versions for development/testing that match Lambda runtime
   - Makes the package immediately usable upon installation
   - Avoids installation warnings or compatibility issues
   - Provides precise version control for our Lambda Layer reference

3. **PeerDependencies benefits**:
   - Explicitly documents dependencies expected in the runtime environment
   - Allows some version flexibility with the semver ranges
   - Signals that these packages are designed to work alongside our bot platform
   - Follows best practices for packages that extend an ecosystem

4. **Special context of `@medplum/bot-layer`**:
   - It's primarily a reference package for Lambda Layer contents
   - It doesn't contain code itself
   - Its purpose is documenting available dependencies for bot developers
   - We want to provide a reliable development environment

5. **Developer experience**:
   - Consistent approach across all packages
   - No surprises about which dependencies are available
   - Clear versioning expectations

## Implementation Requirements

1. Move `@medplum/ccda` and any other packages only in `peerDependencies` to also be in `dependencies`
2. Use exact versions (e.g., "4.1.4") in `dependencies`
3. Maintain semver-compatible versions (e.g., "^4.1.4") in `peerDependencies`
4. Update documentation to explain this approach

## Questions to Consider

- Are there any dependencies that should intentionally be treated differently?
- Are there version conflicts that might arise from this approach?
- Should we have any additional documentation in the package about this pattern?
medplum/medplum#6603.title: Standardize dependency management in `@medplum/bot-layer` package

medplum/medplum#6686.body: ## Problem Summary A customer reported that users are automatically redirected from the `/signin` page to the `/register` page without clicking anything. Investigation revealed: - Issue only occurs for users with the Dashlane Chrome extension installed - The Dashlane extension's auto-login feature programmatically clicks the "Register" link while scanning for forms to autofill - This creates phantom clicks with suspicious properties: - `isTrusted: false` - All coordinates at `(0,0)` - `pointerId: -1` - Customer can work around by disabling Dashlane's "auto-login" feature, but this affects their workflow ## Root Cause Dashlane's form detection logic programmatically interacts with page elements while scanning for login/register forms, inadvertently triggering click handlers on navigation elements. ## Proposed Solutions ### Option 1: Filter Untrusted Events ⭐ **RECOMMENDED** ```javascript const handleRegisterClick = (event) => { if (!event.isTrusted) { console.log('Blocked untrusted click event'); return; } // Normal register logic here }; ``` ### Option 2: Coordinate-Based Detection ```javascript const handleRegisterClick = (event) => { if (event.clientX === 0 && event.clientY === 0 && event.pageX === 0 && event.pageY === 0) { console.log('Blocked phantom click (zero coordinates)'); return; } // Normal register logic here }; ``` ### Option 3: HTML Attributes to Discourage Automation ```html Register ``` ### Option 4: Convert to Button Element ```html ``` ## Recommendation: Option 1 (Untrusted Event Filtering) **Why this is the best approach:** - **Standard Web API**: `event.isTrusted` is specifically designed to distinguish genuine user interactions from programmatic events - **Reliable**: More robust than coordinate checking, which could have false positives - **Future-proof**: Will work against other extensions or automation tools, not just Dashlane - **Well-supported**: Excellent browser compatibility - **Semantically correct**: Uses the web platform as intended ## Implementation Strategy **Don't apply everywhere** - Only add `!event.isTrusted` checks to sensitive click handlers: - ✅ **Navigation triggers** (like Register link) - ✅ **Destructive actions** (delete, logout) - ✅ **Financial/sensitive operations** - ✅ **State-changing actions** (account creation, settings) - ❌ **Form submissions** (extensions should be able to auto-submit) - ❌ **Non-destructive UI actions** (expand/collapse, toggles) - ❌ **User-beneficial automations** **Utility function for reuse:** ```javascript const handleTrustedClick = (callback) => (event) => { if (!event.isTrusted) return; callback(event); }; ``` ## Next Steps 1. Implement `!event.isTrusted` check on the Register link 2. Test with Dashlane extension to confirm fix 3. Identify other sensitive click handlers that should have this protection 4. Create utility function/HOC for consistent implementation across the codebase
medplum/medplum#6686.title: Dashlane Extension Phantom Click Issue

medplum/medplum#6711.body:
## Summary
The JSON Patch `add` operation with path `/member/-` fails when trying to append members to a `Group` resource that has no existing `member` array, throwing `Error: Value required at path: /member/-`.

## Expected Behavior
According to [RFC 6902](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6902), using the `add` operation with path ending in `/-` should append to an array. When the target array doesn't exist, the implementation should handle this gracefully by creating the array and adding the first element.

## Actual Behavior
When attempting to add a member to a Group with no existing members using `/member/-`, the operation fails with:
```
Error: Value required at path: /member/-
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a Group resource with no `member` property
2. Attempt to add a member using JSON Patch with:
   ```json
   {
     "op": "add",
     "path": "/member/-",
     "value": {
       "entity": {
         "reference": "Patient/example"
       }
     }
   }
   ```
3. Operation fails with the error above


## Root Cause Analysis
This appears to be related to FHIR's constraint that empty arrays should be omitted entirely rather than represented as `[]`. This creates a conflict between:

1. **RFC 6902 JSON Patch specification** - expects target array to exist for `/-` append operations
2. **FHIR specification** - requires omitting empty arrays entirely

A Group with no members has no `member` property at all, causing the JSON Patch implementation to fail when trying to append to a non-existent array.

## Proposed Solution
Enhance the JSON Patch implementation to be more FHIR-aware by:

1. **Auto-create arrays for `/-` operations**: When using the `/-` syntax to append to a non-existent array property, automatically create the array and add the first element
2. **Maintain RFC 6902 compliance**: Ensure other JSON Patch operations continue to work as expected
3. **Consistent behavior**: Make the operation work the same whether the Group starts empty or already has members

This would eliminate the need for developers to:
- Pre-check if arrays exist before appending
- Use different patch strategies for first vs. subsequent members
- Manually initialize empty arrays (which violates FHIR spec anyway)

## Current Workarounds

### Option 1: Two-step approach
```javascript
// For first member, use replace operation
await medplum.patchResource('Group', groupId, [
  {
    op: 'replace',
    path: '/member',
    value: [firstMember]
  }
]);

// For subsequent members, use add
await medplum.patchResource('Group', groupId, [
  {
    op: 'add',
    path: '/member/-',
    value: additionalMember
  }
]);
```

### Option 2: Pre-check approach
```javascript
const group = await medplum.readResource('Group', groupId);
if (!group.member || group.member.length === 0) {
  // Use replace for first member
} else {
  // Use add for additional members
}
```

## Impact
This issue affects any code that:
- Dynamically adds members to Groups
- Uses JSON Patch operations on FHIR arrays that might be empty
- Expects consistent behavior from RFC 6902 `/-` append operations

## Additional Notes
- The issue occurs specifically when the array property is completely absent (FHIR-compliant empty state)
- Setting an explicit empty array `[]` before patching doesn't resolve the issue
- The operation works correctly when adding to Groups that already have 1+ members
- This is likely to affect other FHIR resource types with similar array properties
medplum/medplum#6711.title: JSON Patch `add` operation fails when appending to non-existent arrays in FHIR resources

medplum/medplum#6878.body:
# Support for fhirUser claim with fully qualified domain names

## Background

Currently, fhirUser claims can use business identifier queries (e.g., `fhirUser: Patient?identifier=https://.../HCN|123456789`). However, there's a request to support an alternative format for business identifier queries.

## Problem Statement

Internal FHIR IDs are brittle and don't survive FHIR store rebuilds. If we need to reload data from source EMRs (due to synchronization issues, for example), all internal IDs change, breaking existing identity bindings in the identity provider. This would force users to go through the identity verification process again, which is onerous and may involve in-person clinic visits.

## Proposed Enhancement

Add support for an alternative fhirUser claim format that uses business identifiers with full URLs:

**Currently supported formats:**
```
fhirUser: Patient?identifier=https://.../HCN|123456789
```

**Requested format:**
```
fhirUser: https://.../Patient?identifier=HCN|123456789
```

- Original GitHub issue: https://github.com/medplum/medplum/issues/6355
medplum/medplum#6878.title: Support for fhirUser claim with fully qualified domain names

We recently observed a very inefficient query plan being chosen to execute the lateral-join based query. The theory being that the query planner treats the references strings as a generic parameterized query instead of being to see the literal values and therefore falls back to generic selectivity estimates.

A quick proof of concept switching to a `UNION ALL`-based query inspired by our recent obsession with the "ugly OR" problem seems very promising

```sql
SELECT
  "id", "lastUpdated", "content", 'Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' as "ref" FROM "Encounter" WHERE (
  "Encounter"."deleted" = 'f' AND "Encounter"."projectId" IN ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000001111') AND ("Encounter"."appointment" IS NOT NULL AND "Encounter"."appointment" @> ARRAY['Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001']::TEXT[])
)
UNION ALL
SELECT "Encounter"."id", "Encounter"."lastUpdated", "Encounter"."content", 'Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002' as "ref" FROM "Encounter" WHERE (
  "Encounter"."deleted" = 'f' AND "Encounter"."projectId" IN ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000001111') AND ("Encounter"."appointment" IS NOT NULL AND "Encounter"."appointment" @> ARRAY['Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002']::TEXT[])
)
UNION ALL
SELECT "Encounter"."id", "Encounter"."lastUpdated", "Encounter"."content", 'Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003' as "ref" FROM "Encounter" WHERE (
  "Encounter"."deleted" = 'f' AND "Encounter"."projectId" IN ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000001111') AND ("Encounter"."appointment" IS NOT NULL AND "Encounter"."appointment" @> ARRAY['Appointment/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003']::TEXT[])
);
UNION ALL
...
```

medplum/medplum#6958.body:
## Problem

Since old versions of the agent relied on the previous running agent shutting themselves down before the upgrader process ran the installer to ensure ports were free, downgrading to a non-zero-downtime agent via `Agent/$upgrade` operation from a zero-downtime agent (where the service is not stopped before running the installer) results in:

1. Zero-downtime agent running and remaining bound to channel ports
2. Old non-zero-downtime agent running, either fighting for the PID file OR (worse) fighting for ports and potentially crash-looping

## Proposed solution

When running the `Agent/$upgrade` operation with a specified version less than 4.2.4:

1. Without `--force`, we error when attempting to upgrade to a version before 4.2.4 , warning that this could be bad and cause service outage since it pre-dates zero-downtime upgrade. "Invoke the command again with `--force` to stop and uninstall the current agent and install agent with version X"
2. With `--force`, upgrader stops and uninstalls current agent service before running the installer for the pre-4.2.4 version, which would allow for downgrade but with some downtime while the service is starting up

This would basically follow the old pattern before zero-downtime upgrade feature.
medplum/medplum#6958.title: Downgrading to pre-zero-downtime agent (pre-4.2.4) from post-4.2.4 results in two running services

medplum/medplum#6974.body:
We have started using the taxonomy-server feature of medplum, alongside the valueset expansion feature and it seems there are an index that is missing.

Our query insights are reporting a sequential scan on "Coding_Property" from this query. The coding system has been populated using the `CodeSystem/$import` operation with ~ 1800 items for one and ~200000 for another codesystem.

Medplum Version 4.1.7
Database: Postgres 15.12

```sql
SELECT
  "Coding"."id",
  "Coding"."code",
  "Coding"."display"
FROM
  "Coding"
WHERE
  ("Coding"."system" = $1
    AND EXISTS(
    SELECT
      $4
    FROM
      "Coding_Property"
    INNER JOIN
      "CodeSystem_Property" AS "T1"
    ON
      ("T1"."id" = "Coding_Property"."property"
        AND "T1"."code" = $2)
    WHERE
      ("Coding"."id" = "Coding_Property"."coding"
        AND "value" = $3)))
LIMIT
  $5
```

After adding this index, query execution time dropped from 500ms to 20ms on this one.

```sql
CREATE INDEX "Fluent_Health_Coding_Property_coding_property_idx" ON "Coding_Property" (coding, property);
```
medplum/medplum#6974.title: Taxonomy Server ValueSet Expand Performance

medplum/medplum#6986.body:
# Enable support for multiple ProjectMembership resources for the same User+Project

See [this thread](https://medplum.slack.com/archives/C06TDV97JPM/p1751906959885599?thread_ts=1751581105.954059&cid=C06TDV97JPM) for more info

## Problem

Currently, the system prevents the creation of multiple ProjectMembership resources for the same User+Project pair. This limitation blocks legitimate use cases where:

Administrative users need to access different tenant contexts within the same project, which would require different `ProjectMembership.profile` or `ProjectMembership.access` for each tenant that they can access.

The current workaround would be continuously updating a single ProjectMembership resource, but this is not an advisable solution.

## Proposed Solution
1. Allow Multiple ProjectMemberships per User+Project Pair

- Add a `force` parameter to the `/admin/invite` endpoint to allow intentional creation of multiple memberships, while maintaining existing behavior as default to prevent accidental duplicates

2. Enhanced Token Exchange Flow

- Add optional parameter to token-exchange flow to specify which ProjectMembership to use
- Enable explicit selection of a ProjectMembership to select during authentication

3. Default Behavior

- When multiple ProjectMemberships exist and none is explicitly specified, default to the most recently used ProjectMembership
- Get rid of  current error behavior that occurs when multiple memberships are encountered
- Base "most recent" determination on the most recent Login record for that User+Project pair
medplum/medplum#6986.title: Support for multiple ProjectMembership resources for the same User+Project

* Establish secure default deployment behavior
* Ensure basic resource policies are in place
* Remove dangerous or brittle placeholder values

  * Drop unnecessary capabilities

### 📝 Acceptance Criteria

* Default chart configuration deploys securely to a test cluster without requiring user intervention
* `helm lint` passes cleanly
* No hardcoded placeholder values remain in the default chart
* Pods run with basic security contexts applied
* All core workloads have resource limits and PDBs defined

medplum/medplum#7110.body:
As discussed in today's engineering sync...

## Overview

We're proposing to extract two packages related to Expo and React Native from our main monorepo into their own dedicated repositories. This change aims to streamline our development process, reduce build complexity, and better align with our focus as a healthcare developer platform.

The packages to be moved are:

1.  **`@medplum/expo-polyfills`**
    * **Current Path:** `packages/expo-polyfills`
    * **Purpose:** Our collection of Expo and React Native polyfills, enabling Medplum's core libraries to function on Expo and React Native environments.
    * **New Repository:** `medplum/medplum-expo-polyfills`

2.  **`medplum-react-native-example`**
    * **Current Path:** `examples/medplum-react-native-example`
    * **Purpose:** A demo application showcasing the use of `@medplum/expo-polyfills` within an Expo/React Native app.
    * **New Repository:** `medplum/medplum-react-native-example`

---

## Rationale

This extraction addresses several challenges we currently face by keeping these mobile-focused packages within our monorepo:

1.  **Infrequent Changes:** Expo and React Native environments evolve at a slower pace. These packages are updated approximately once per quarter, making their presence in our daily-use monorepo inefficient.
2.  **Complex Upgrade Process:** Expo and React Native upgrades are notoriously fragile and require synchronized updates across numerous dependencies. This often leads to brittle and time-consuming maintenance within our existing setup.
3.  **CI/CD Overhead:** Testing Expo and React Native applications in our CI/CD pipeline demands complex build chains, adding unnecessary overhead and resource consumption to our primary development flow.
4.  **Dependency Upgrade Conflicts:** Our aggressive weekly dependency upgrade policy, while beneficial for most packages, exacerbates the issues described above for Expo and React Native, leading to frequent breaking changes and debugging.
5.  **Limited Mobile Focus:** As a healthcare developer platform, our core expertise isn't in building native mobile applications. While we provide mobile support as a service, addressing deep Android/iOS native issues can be challenging and time-consuming for our team to resolve promptly within the monorepo's rapid development cycle.
6.  **Monorepo Bloat:** Expo and React Native dependencies are substantial, accounting for over 25% of our `node_modules` size and file count. This significantly increases installation and reinstallation times for developers who don't directly work on mobile, impacting overall developer experience and efficiency.

---

## Next Steps

We will begin by creating the new repositories and then facilitate the migration of the codebases and their respective histories. medplum/medplum#7133.body:
We currently uses **GitHub Actions** for CI/CD, which requires **AWS credentials** to interact with our AWS environment. Our **SOC 2 Type 2 compliance** via Vanta mandates that all AWS credentials be rotated every **90 days**.

Currently, this rotation is a manual process. This creates a recurring chore every 90 days.

### Proposed Solution: Implement OpenID Connect (OIDC) for AWS Credential Management

To automate AWS credential rotation and enhance our security posture, we should implement **OpenID Connect (OIDC)** integration between GitHub Actions and AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM). This approach eliminates the need for long-lived static AWS access keys stored in GitHub Secrets and automatically handles credential rotation.

### Benefits of OIDC

* **Automated Credential Rotation:** OIDC provides **short-lived, temporary AWS credentials** for each GitHub Actions workflow run. These credentials expire automatically, inherently satisfying the 90-day rotation requirement without any manual intervention.
* **Enhanced Security:**
    * **No Stored Secrets:** Eliminates the storage of sensitive, long-lived AWS access keys in GitHub Secrets, significantly reducing the risk of compromise.
    * **Least Privilege:** Allows us to define highly specific IAM roles with only the necessary permissions, limiting the blast radius if a workflow were ever compromised.
    * **Fine-Grained Access Control:** We can configure IAM trust policies to permit only specific GitHub repositories, branches, or workflows to assume designated roles.
* **SOC 2 Type 2 Compliance:** The use of ephemeral credentials directly addresses and simplifies compliance with Vanta's requirements for regular credential rotation, demonstrating a mature security control.
* **Improved Auditability:** AWS CloudTrail logs will record every instance of an IAM role being assumed by GitHub Actions, providing a clear and comprehensive audit trail.
* **Reduced Operational Overhead:** Eliminates the manual, recurring chore of updating AWS credentials every 90 days, freeing up engineering time.

### Implementation Steps (High-Level)

1.  **Configure an IAM OIDC Provider in AWS:** Set up `token.actions.githubusercontent.com` as an OIDC provider in our AWS account.
2.  **Create Specific IAM Roles:** Define new IAM roles for our GitHub Actions workflows. These roles will have:
    * A trust policy allowing the OIDC provider (GitHub) to assume them, with conditions to restrict access to specific GitHub repositories/branches.
    * Only the **minimum necessary permissions** attached for our CI/CD operations (e.g., S3 deployment, ECS updates, etc.).
3.  **Update GitHub Actions Workflows:** Modify existing GitHub Actions workflows to use the `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` action. This action will leverage the OIDC provider to assume the appropriate IAM role and obtain temporary credentials for the workflow run.
medplum/medplum#7133.title: Automate AWS Credential Rotation for GitHub Actions via OIDC

medplum/medplum#7253.body:
### Description
There is an inconsistency in how the `storageBaseUrl` is generated between the `medplum aws init` and `medplum aws update-config` commands, which results in incorrect configuration being stored in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store.

### Bug Details
- **`medplum aws init`** [code](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/30e6fcb38cd92d945acc1af3e6df231e2281d21d/packages/cli/src/aws/init.ts#L247) generates:
  ```typescript
  storageBaseUrl: `https://${config.storageDomainName}/binary/`
  ```

- **`medplum aws update-config`** [code](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/30e6fcb38cd92d945acc1af3e6df231e2281d21d/packages/cli/src/aws/update-config.ts#L121) generates:
  ```typescript
  serverConfig.storageBaseUrl = `https://${infraConfig.storageDomainName}/`;
  ```

### Impact
This inconsistency causes the `storageBaseUrl` parameter in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store to be missing the `/binary/` path component when using the `update-config` command. This can lead to:
- Broken file upload/download functionality
- Incorrect binary resource URLs
- API calls failing to locate storage resources

### Expected Behavior
Both commands should generate the same `storageBaseUrl` format with the `/binary/` path component:
```
https://${storageDomainName}/binary/
```

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `medplum aws init` to create initial configuration
2. Observe that the `storageBaseUrl` in Parameter Store includes `/binary/`
3. Run `medplum aws update-config` to update the configuration
4. After accepting the prompt about the differences in values, observe that the `storageBaseUrl` in Parameter Store now lacks the `/binary/` path

### Suggested Fix
Update `packages/cli/src/aws/update-config.ts` from:
```typescript
serverConfig.storageBaseUrl = `https://${infraConfig.storageDomainName}/`;
```
to:
```typescript
serverConfig.storageBaseUrl = `https://${infraConfig.storageDomainName}/binary/`;
```
Update unit tests

### Environment
- **Medplum Version**: Latest (main branch)
- **Affected Files**: 
  - `packages/cli/src/aws/init.ts`
  - `packages/cli/src/aws/update-config.ts`

### Additional Context
This appears to be a simple oversight where the `/binary/` path was omitted in the `update-config` implementation. The `init` command correctly includes this path component as documented in the [AWS installation guide](https://www.medplum.com/docs/self-hosting/install-on-aws).

I will submit a PR for this shortly.
medplum/medplum#7253.title: Bug: Inconsistent storageBaseUrl generation between aws init and aws update-config commands

medplum/medplum#7373.body:
## Description

There is a bug in the `QuestionnaireForm` when handling repeatable groups. When a `Questionnaire` with repeatable groups is submitted, the generated `QuestionnaireResponse` does not correctly capture multiple answers. Although multiple group instances are created in the response, only the last submitted answer contains data, while the others remain empty.

### Root cause

The `getResponseItemByContext` function in `useQuestionnaireForm.ts `only uses linkId to locate response items. For repeatable groups, multiple instances share the same `linkId`, which causes overwriting.

### Proposed solution

Update `getResponseItemByContext` to use the unique `id` field when available, falling back to `linkId` otherwise.

## Steps to test

1. Create a `Questionnaire` with a repeatable group.
2. Submit the `Questionnaire` with at least two answers in the repeatable group.
3. Inspect the `QuestionnaireResponse` → multiple entries are created, but only one contains data, and the others remain empty.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/396536a2-dd40-4e7a-80df-d2db987c3a43

medplum/medplum#7391.body: Related: https://github.com/medplum/medplum/pull/6760 ## Problem When I try to add [this StructureDefinition](http://fhir.de/StructureDefinition/informationrecipient) to medplum, I get an error. The same error happens with many StructureDefinitions inside the [de.basisprofil.r4](https://simplifier.net/packages/de.basisprofil.r4/1.5.4) package that use differentials. `Constraint sdf-8a not met: In any differential, all the elements must start with the StructureDefinition's specified type for non-logical models, or with the same type name for logical models ({"fhirpath":"(%resource.kind = 'logical' or element.first().path.startsWith(%resource.type)) and (element.tail().empty() or element.tail().all(path.startsWith(%resource.differential.element.first().path.replaceMatches('\\\\..*','')&'.')))"}) (StructureDefinition.differential)` ## Deep Dive After doing some research, I found the following: The FHIRPath in [sdf-8a](https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/structuredefinition-definitions.html) uses `replaceMatches('\\..*','')` to turn e.g. `"Extension.url"` into `"Extension"`. This transformation however fails in the current @medplum/core, because it internally creates the regex `/\\..*/` instead of `/\..*/`. ## Try it out ```ts test('Test replaceMatches()', () => { expect(evalFhirPath(`foo.replaceMatches('\\\\..*','')`, { foo: 'Extension.url' })).toStrictEqual(['Extension']); // ❌ Fails currently // ^ Why 4 backslashes? // Because the FHIRPath expression for sdf-8a is literally \\..* // That's why we need to escape them in the javascript string // Instead of '\\\\..*' we could also write String.raw`\\..*` }); ``` ## Solution(?) My first thought would be to manually remove the "over-escaped" backslashes. But I'm not sure if that's the right place to make the change. It could also be a bug upstream, and the [source file](https://simplifier.net/packages/hl7.fhir.r4.core/4.0.1/files/2831303/~xml) should actually say `\..*` instead of `\\..*`. But someone else should be the judge of that. https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/5db6a9472dd897cd8c5fe3d9e355577042dba314/packages/core/src/fhirpath/functions.ts#L1301 ```diff - (str, pattern, substition) => str.replaceAll(new RegExp(pattern, 'g'), substition) + (str, pattern, substition) => str.replaceAll(new RegExp(pattern.replaceAll('\\\\', '\\'), 'g'), substition) ```
medplum/medplum#7391.title: Cannot add StructureDefinition. Problem with FHIRPath replaceMatches()

medplum/medplum#7476.body:
The Postgres query planner's selectivity estimates for filters on array columns can vastly overestimate how many rows are expected to match the filter. This can cause Postgres to choose a suboptimal query plan, even sequential scans, rather than utilizing the available indexes on the column.  This leads to significantly slower and more expensive queries and system instability. See #7310 for a more in-depth technical analysis.

To address this in the short term, an opt-in `@medplum/server` feature is added. When enabled, the shape of the SQL queries generated to fulfill certain FHIR searches will we changed to encourage the query planner to utilize indexes rather than resorting to expensive sequential scans. The results of the FHIR search will remain unchanged.

Based on current research, this feature will have two effects.

First, it will strongly discourage sequential scans by setting `enable_seqscan = off` for the FHIR search's Postgres query. Despite the name, this change doesn't truly remove the possibiilty of a sequential scan being used; it just massively inflates the cost of a sequential scan to the planner.

Second, enforce a artificially higher floor in the `LIMIT N` clause included in the generated SQL queries. Why? When they query planner vastly over estimates how many matching rows exist and we are only asking for a couple rows to be returned, e.g. `LIMIT 2`, it chooses sequential scan since it thinks it will surely find those two rows without needing to scan much of the table. Increasing the limit has a dramatic impact on the planner's estimated cost of the sequential scan which makes it more likely for an alternative, index-based plan to be used.
medplum/medplum#7476.title: Discourage sequential scans in FHIR searches

medplum/medplum#7539.body:
The idea is to add extra, well-known elements to array columns that fall victim to the quirk in Postgres array column statistics discussed in https://github.com/medplum/medplum/issues/7310 as well as on a [pg mailing list thread](https://www.postgresql.org/message-id/flat/PH3PPF1C905D6E6F24A5C1A1A1D8345B593E16FA%40PH3PPF1C905D6E6.namprd15.prod.outlook.com).

Some initial thoughts and math on what this would look like.

The formula for minimum frequency for consideration into the MCE list:

```c
/*
 * We want statistics_target * 10 elements in the MCELEM array. This
 * multiplier is pretty arbitrary, but is meant to reflect the fact that
 * the number of individual elements tracked in pg_statistic ought to be
 * more than the number of values for a simple scalar column.
 */
num_mcelem = stats->attr->attstattarget * 10;

/*
 * We set bucket width equal to num_mcelem / 0.007 as per the comment
 * above.
 */
bucket_width = num_mcelem * 1000 / 7;

/*
 * Construct an array of the interesting hashtable items, that is,
 * those meeting the cutoff frequency (s - epsilon)*N.  Also identify
 * the minimum and maximum frequencies among these items.
 *
 * Since epsilon = s/10 and bucket_width = 1/epsilon, the cutoff
 * frequency is 9*N / bucket_width.
 */
cutoff_freq = 9 * element_no / bucket_width;
```

For the identifier scenario where each row has an array with a common element and a unique element.

```
element_no = 2 * row_count
bucket_width = stats_target * 10 * 1000 / 7
cutoff_freq = ⌈9 * (2 * row_count) / (stats_target * 10 * 1000 / 7)⌉
```

With a statistics target of 100, the `ANALYZE` command samples `R` = 100 * 300 = 30,000 rows. In the “too unique” identifiers scenario, each row of the array column contains a common array element and a unique element. The array column sample contains 60,000 total elements `n` across the sampled `R` rows; 2 elements per row. For an element to be included in most_common_elements (MCE), it must appear at least `k` times where:

`k = ⌈9 * 2 * 30000 /142,857⌉ ≈ ⌈3.78⌉ = 4`

This guarantees that none of the unique elements will appear in the MCE since they definitionally will not appear more than once in a given sample. The result is an MCE consisting of only the common element and the query planner using the fallback `minfreq` value of `DEFAULT_CONTAIN_SEL = 0.005` when querying for a unique elements; a vast over-estimate resulting in sequential scans, particularly when querying for just a few rows, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "Patient" WHERE __identifier @> ARRAY[<unique-value>] LIMIT 2`.

To avoid this, randomly add each of `m` well-known, artificial values into a fraction `f` of all rows such that at least one such well-known value qualifies for the MCE with a fequency `S` close to but above `k` with high confidence `p`. Meaning each time `ANALYZE` runs,  a  `minfreq` much lower than the default `0.005` is computed resulting in more accurate row estimates when querying for unique elements. For each `m` to occur with frequency `f`, add a random value from `[0, m)` to the array column with probability `m*f/30000`. Assuming a random sample, the count of these well-known values in the sample follows a binomial distribution, which we approximate as Poisson for small `f`.

**Definitions:**

Let X = count of well-known values in sample
X ~ Binomial(30,000, f) ≈ Poisson(λ) where λ = 30,000f

**Constraints:**

`k` can be treated as a constant 4 due to ceiling operation.

**Problem statement:**

For m distinct values, we want the probability that at least one m exceeds frequency k in the sample with probability p.

**Solution:**

Using the Poisson CDF, `P(X ≥ 4) ≥ p` or  `P(X ≥ 4) = 1 - P(X ≤ 3) ≥ p`
Over all m, `1 - P(X ≤ 3)^m ≥ p`
Rewrite as `P(X ≤ 3) ≤ (1-p)^(1/m)`

**Example calculation for m = 10, p = 0.9999:**

- P(X ≤ 3) ≤ (1-0.9999)^(1/10)
- P(X ≤ 3) ≤ 0.39810717
- Minimal λ to satisfy is ~4.2
- f = λ/30,000 = 4.2/30,000 = 0.00014

**Impact on column size**

Depending on `m` and `p`, extra array elements are added to 0.25% to 0.5% of rows; 250-500 array elements per 100,000 rows.

**λ(m, p) calculations**

m | p | P(X ≤ 3) ≤ | ~λ | m*f
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
10 | 0.9999 | 0.3981071706 | 4.3 | 0.14%
10 | 0.99999 | 0.316227766 | 4.7 | 0.16%
20 | 0.99999 | 0.5623413252 | 3.4 | 0.23%
20 | 0.999999 | 0.5011872336 | 3.7 | 0.25%
40 | 0.99999 | 0.7498942093 | 2.6 | 0.35%
40 | 0.999999 | 0.7079457844 | 2.8 | 0.37%
60 | 0.99999 | 0.8254041853 | 2.2 | 0.44%
60 | 0.999999 | 0.7943282347 | 2.3 | 0.46%
80 | 0.99999 | 0.8659643234 | 2 | 0.53%
80 | 0.999999 | 0.8413951416 | 2.1 | 0.56%

_[Supporting spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PTHzKAdPshA7czBhmncesDCZGXkb8fv3jG3qZyEoLVs/edit?usp=sharing) for Poisson calculations_

**Create a simple test table**
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.test(
    id integer,
    tags text[]
);
```

**Populate test table for a given m and lambda**
```sql
DO $$
DECLARE
  m integer := 20;
  lambda decimal := 3.7;
BEGIN
TRUNCATE TABLE public.test; INSERT INTO public.test (id, tags)
  SELECT n,
	  CASE
		  WHEN random() <= (m*lambda::decimal/30000) THEN  ARRAY['common', TO_CHAR(n,'fm00000000'), 'wellknown_' || floor(random()*m)::text]
		  ELSE ARRAY['common', TO_CHAR(n,'fm00000000')]
		END FROM ( SELECT generate_series(1,1_000_000) AS n );
END $$;
```

**Analye table once and show relevant statistics**
```sql
ANALYZE test;
SELECT
  most_common_elem_freqs[array_upper(most_common_elem_freqs,1)-2] as minfreq,
  array_length(most_common_elems, 1) as mce_count
FROM pg_stats
WHERE tablename = 'test' AND attname in ('tags');
```

**Script to repeatedly analyze table and gather results**
```bash
#!/bin/bash

N=500
psql -h localhost -d postgres <<EOF
set client_min_messages = notice;
CREATE TEMP TABLE analyze_results (
  iteration int,
  minfreq numeric,
  mce_count int
);
DO \$\$
BEGIN
  FOR i IN 1..$N LOOP
    ANALYZE test;
    INSERT INTO analyze_results
    SELECT
      i,
      most_common_elem_freqs[array_upper(most_common_elem_freqs,1)-2],
      array_length(most_common_elems, 1)
    FROM pg_stats
    WHERE tablename = 'test' AND attname = 'tags';
  END LOOP;
END \$\$;

SELECT minfreq, count(*) FROM analyze_results GROUP BY minfreq ORDER BY minfreq;
SELECT minfreq, mce_count, count(*) FROM analyze_results GROUP BY minfreq, mce_count ORDER BY minfreq, mce_count;
EOF
```

**Sample output for m = 20, p=0.999999, lambda = 3.7**
```

-------------+-------

Time: 0.805 ms

In the past, account was a single reference and the check for agreement between the `meta.account` for the `Subscription` and the evaluated resource was a valid way to enforce multi-tenant isolation within a project. However, we now support the plural `meta.accounts` which is an array and `meta.account` is populated by the first entry in `meta.accounts`.

On top of that, we also support using the `_compartment` search parameter, which includes all `meta.accounts` references. This is now the canonical way to do tenant isolation.

This is a breaking change, but we should use the upcoming v5 release to remove this functionality, as it is already only used by 1 or 2 customers that we know of based on the logs we added in #7561.

EDIT: Originally we were planning to remove `meta.account` filtering altogether, however we decided that it may be easier to  normalize the `meta.account` and `meta.accounts` first, using an existing util `extractAccountReferences` from `@medplum/server` that we move to `@medplum/core`.

medplum/medplum#7649.body:
Currently, it is possible to delete a `ProfileResource` (Patient, Practitioner, or RelatedPerson) even if there are active `ProjectMembership` resources associated with it. This can lead  inconsistencies within the system that affect login.

**Proposal**
We would like to enhance the deletion logic to prevent the deletion of a `ProfileResource` if there is at least one `ProjectMembership` resource that references it. When a user attempts to delete such a `ProfileResource`, the system should return an error (e.g., an OperationOutcome) indicating that the resource cannot be deleted due to existing `ProjectMembership` references.

To avoid breaking existing customers, this functionality should be controlled by a new **project-level setting**, for example, `preventProfileDeletionWithMembership`.


This can be seen as a special case of a broader future feature to prevent the deletion of *any* resource that has a reference pointing to it, thus enforcing referential integrity across the FHIR data store.

*   **Default Value:** `false` for existing projects and `true` for newly created projects. This allows existing customers to opt-in at their convenience, while new projects benefit from enhanced data integrity from the start.

**Impact on `auth/me`:**

The `/auth/me` endpoint, retrieves the user's ProjectMembership and then tries to read the associated profile resource:
If the ProfileResource has been deleted, systemRepo.readReference will fail, causing a server error and preventing the user from accessing the application.


*   **Current Deletion Logic and Referential Integrity:**
    The core `deleteResource` method is located in the `Repository` class.
    [https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/server/src/fhir/repo.ts#L1173](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/server/src/fhir/repo.ts#L1173)

    Currently, referential integrity is explicitly *not* supported for deletes, as noted in the documentation:
    [https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/docs/docs/fhir-datastore/deleting-data.md#L30-L34](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/docs/docs/fhir-datastore/deleting-data.md#L30-L34)
medplum/medplum#7649.title: Prevent Deletion of ProfileResources with Active ProjectMemberships

medplum/medplum#7651.body: The autodownloader feature scans resources for external URL's, and adds them to a queue that attempts to download the content of these URL's into Medplum binary storage. If `n` external URL's are present in the same resource, each of those URL's are added to the job queue separately. Upon the content being downloaded, the autodownloader updates the whole resource. This causes a race condition where each of the jobs overwrites the work of other jobs, and the resulting resources are queued into the autodownloader again, until eventually `n` jobs write non-external URLs. Relevant file with updateResource line: https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/server/src/workers/download.ts#L242 Example resource where we observed this behavior: https://app.medplum.com/DocumentReference/fb6a6d35-ff7f-4628-bf41-3a92f02d7178/history Relevant cursor chat: [cursor_is_autodownload_enabled_for_pres.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23031368/cursor_is_autodownload_enabled_for_pres.md)
medplum/medplum#7651.title: Autodownloader race condition when resource contains multiple presigned URLs

medplum/medplum#7686.body:
## Overview

Implement the FHIR `$find` operation for searching available appointment slots, based on the [[IHE ITI.Scheduling specification](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-find.html)](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-find.html) with Medplum-specific enhancements.

## Endpoints

The operation is available on three resource types, returning different response formats:

- **`[base]/Appointment/$find`** - Returns `Bundle` of proposed `Appointment` resources

All three share the same input parameters and processing logic, differing only in response format.

## MVP Scope

**Version 1 includes:**
- `start`, `end` - date range (required)
- `schedule` - explicit Schedule reference(s) (required for MVP)
- `service-type` - filter by service type
- `patient-reference` - patient context
- Support for multi-schedule intersection queries
- Buffer time calculations
- Booking limit enforcement
- Alignment interval/offset handling

**Out of scope for Version 1** (future enhancements):
- Schedule inference from `practitioner`, `organization`, `location-reference`, `location-string`
- `specialty` filtering
- `reason` codes
- `service-request` references  
- `coverage` information
- `preferred-times` optimization
- `patient-resource` for new patients

For MVP, clients must explicitly provide `schedule` parameter(s). Schedule discovery from other parameters will be added in follow-up PRs.

## Input Parameters

### Required Parameters

**`start`** (dateTime, 1..1) - *IHE Standard*
- Start of availability search period
- Must be in ISO 8601 format with timezone

**`end`** (dateTime, 1..1) - *IHE Standard*
- End of availability search period
- Must be in ISO 8601 format with timezone

**`schedule`** (string, 1..*) - *Medplum Extension*
- Direct Schedule resource reference(s)
- Format: `Schedule/{id}`
- Multiple schedules calculate availability intersection (all must be available)
- Example: `schedule=Schedule/dr-smith` or `schedule=Schedule/surgeon&schedule=Schedule/or-3`

### Optional Parameters

**`service-type`** (string, 0..*) - *IHE Standard*
- Service type codes in format `[system]|[code]`
- Used to select which scheduling-parameters extension applies
- Multiple values treated as OR (any match)

**`patient-reference`** (string, 0..1) - *IHE Standard*
- Patient reference for context
- Format: `Patient/{id}`

## Processing Logic

### 1. Schedule Validation

- Verify all `schedule` references exist and are active
- Filter to schedules matching `service-type` if provided
- Return error if no valid schedules remain

### 2. Load Scheduling Parameters

For each Schedule:

**Determine which scheduling-parameters to use:**

Priority order (highest to lowest):
- Schedule + service-specific
- ActivityDefinition + service-specific
- Schedule + default (no serviceType)

1. Look for `scheduling-parameters` extensions on the Schedule
2. If `service-type` is provided in the request:
   - Find the `scheduling-parameters` extension that has a `serviceType` sub-extension matching the requested service type
   - If found, use this extension exclusively (all-or-nothing override)
   - If not found, fall back to step 3
3. If no service-type-specific override exists on Schedule:
   - load defaults from ActivityDefinition where `ActivityDefinition.code` matches the requested `service-type`
   - If this doesn't exist, Use the generic `scheduling-parameters` extension on Schedule (one without `serviceType` sub-extension)
4. Extract: `availability`, `bufferBefore`, `bufferAfter`, `alignmentInterval`, `alignmentOffset`, `bookingLimit`, `timingDuration`

### 3. Calculate Availability Windows

**Generate base availability:**
- Parse `availability.valueTiming.repeat` to generate recurring windows
- Apply `dayOfWeek`, `timeOfDay`, `duration` within search period
- Handle timezone conversions from Schedule or actor

**For multi-schedule queries:**
- Calculate intersection of all availability windows
- Only return times where ALL schedules are simultaneously available

### 4. Query Existing Slots

Find Slot resources for each Schedule where:
- `Slot.schedule` references the Schedule
- `Slot.start >= {search start}` AND `Slot.end <= {search end}` (use Medplum's `Slot.end` search parameter)

Three slot types affect availability:

**`status=busy`** - Booked appointments
- Block the time completely
- Apply `bufferBefore` and `bufferAfter` around the slot

**`status=busy-unavailable`** - Explicit blocks
- Block the time completely  
- Apply `bufferBefore` and `bufferAfter` around the slot

**`status=free`** - Explicit availability overrides
- Mark time as available even if outside normal `availability` windows
- Useful for adding extra hours or one-time availability
- These slots represent actual FHIR resources (unlike the virtual slots returned by $find)

### 5. Apply Constraints

- Remove time occupied by `busy` and `busy-unavailable` Slots with buffers
- Add time from `free` Slots (overrides unavailable periods)
- Check `bookingLimit` constraints (e.g., max 3 appointments per day)
- Exclude windows that would violate booking limits

### 6. Generate Available Slots

- Use `alignmentInterval` to determine valid start times (e.g., every 15 min: :00, :15, :30, :45)
- Apply `alignmentOffset` to shift grid (e.g., offset 5 min → :05, :20, :35, :50)
- Use `ActivityDefinition.timingDuration` for slot duration
- Create virtual slots for each available window

## Response Format`Appointment/$find` Response

Returns proposed Appointment resources with pre-populated participants (no resource IDs or slot references):

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Bundle",
  "type": "searchset",
  "entry": [
    {
      "resource": {
        "resourceType": "Appointment",
        "status": "proposed",
        "serviceType": [{"coding": [{"code": "office-visit"}]}],
        "start": "2025-10-28T09:00:00-07:00",
        "end": "2025-10-28T09:30:00-07:00",
        "participant": [
          {
            "actor": {"reference": "Practitioner/dr-smith"},
            "required": "required",
            "status": "needs-action"
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```



### Multi-Schedule Response

**For `Appointment/$find`:**
- Return one Appointment per time window
- Include all required participants

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Appointment",
  "status": "proposed",
  "start": "2025-11-01T08:30:00-07:00",
  "end": "2025-11-01T10:30:00-07:00",
  "participant": [
    {"actor": {"reference": "PractitionerRole/surgeon-martinez"}, "required": "required"},
    {"actor": {"reference": "Location/or-3"}, "required": "required"},
    {"actor": {"reference": "PractitionerRole/anesthesiologist-kim"}, "required": "required"}
  ]
}
```

## Edge Cases

1. **No availability**: Return empty Bundle with OperationOutcome information
2. **Invalid schedule references**: Return OperationOutcome error
4. **Booking limits exceeded**: Exclude windows violating frequency limits
5. **Conflicting free/busy slots**: `busy` and `busy-unavailable` always take precedence over `free`
6. **Multi-schedule partial availability**: Only return windows where ALL schedules are free

## Examples

### Single Schedule Query
```
GET [base]/Slot/$find?
  start=2025-10-28T00:00:00Z&
  end=2025-10-31T23:59:59Z&
  schedule=Schedule/dr-johnson
```

### Multi-Resource Surgery
```
GET [base]/Appointment/$find?
  start=2025-11-01T00:00:00Z&
  end=2025-11-30T23:59:59Z&
  schedule=Schedule/surgeon-martinez&
  schedule=Schedule/or-3&
  schedule=Schedule/anesthesiologist-kim
```

### Service Type Filter
```
GET [base]/Slot/$find?
  start=2025-11-01T00:00:00Z&
  end=2025-11-05T23:59:59Z&
  schedule=Schedule/dr-chen&
  service-type=http://example.org|new-patient-visit
```

### Context-based (Single Schedule)
```
GET [base]/Schedule/dr-smith-schedule/$find?
  start=2025-10-28T00:00:00Z&
  end=2025-10-31T23:59:59Z
```

## Implementation Notes

- Virtual resources (Slots/Appointments) should NOT be persisted - calculated on demand
- Virtual resources do NOT have resource IDs
- Support both GET and POST syntax per FHIR conventions
- 
## Testing Scenarios
1. Single schedule with ActivityDefinition defaults
2. Schedule with `scheduling-parameters` override for service type
3. Multi-schedule intersection (surgical)
4. Booking limits preventing appointments
5. Buffer times around existing busy Slots
6. Alignment intervals and offsets
7. Explicit `free` Slots adding availability
8. Conflicting free/busy slots (busy wins)
9. All three endpoint types (Slot, Appointment, Schedule)
10. Empty results with OperationOutcome

## Related Resources

- [[Medplum Scheduling Data Model](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/docs/docs/scheduling/data-model.md)](https://github.com/medplum/medplum/blob/main/packages/docs/docs/scheduling/data-model.md)
- [[IHE ITI.Scheduling $find](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-find.html)](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-find.html)
medplum/medplum#7686.title: $find Operation for Appointment Scheduling

medplum/medplum#7690.body:
#
## Overview

Implement the FHIR `Appointment/$book` operation for booking appointments, based on the [[IHE ITI.Scheduling specification](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-book.html)](https://build.fhir.org/ig/IHE/ITI.Scheduling/OperationDefinition-appointment-book.html).

## Endpoints

- **`[base]/Appointment/$book`** - Book appointments

## MVP Scope

**Version 1 includes:**
- Book from `$hold` results (convert tentative to booked)
- Book from `$find` results (direct booking without hold)
- Multi-schedule atomic booking

**Out of scope for Version 1:**
- Modify existing appointments
- Cancel appointments (separate `$cancel` operation)
- Patient resource parameter
- Comment parameter

## Input Parameters

**`appointment`** (Appointment, 1..1) - *IHE Standard / Medplum Modified*
- From `$hold`: Must have `status=pending` with `slot` references
- From `$find`: Must have `status=proposed` with `participant` actors
- Must include `start`, `end`, `serviceType`, and participant information

## Processing Logic

### Scenario 1: Book from Hold (status=pending)

**Input validation:**
- Appointment has `status=pending`
- All `slot` references exist with `status=busy-tentative`

**Processing:**
Use FHIR transaction bundle to atomically:
- PATCH all slots: `busy-tentative` → `busy`
- PATCH appointment: `pending` → `booked`

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Bundle",
  "type": "transaction",
  "entry": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "PATCH",
        "url": "Slot/tentative-slot-1"
      },
      "resource": {
        "resourceType": "Parameters",
        "parameter": [
          {"name": "operation", "part": [
            {"name": "type", "valueCode": "replace"},
            {"name": "path", "valueString": "Slot.status"},
            {"name": "value", "valueCode": "busy"}
          ]}
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "PATCH",
        "url": "Appointment/held-appointment-123"
      },
      "resource": {
        "resourceType": "Parameters",
        "parameter": [
          {"name": "operation", "part": [
            {"name": "type", "valueCode": "replace"},
            {"name": "path", "valueString": "Appointment.status"},
            {"name": "value", "valueCode": "booked"}
          ]}
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Scenario 2: Book from $find (status=proposed, no hold)

**Processing follows same logic as `$hold` operation** (see https://github.com/medplum/medplum/issues/7688 ):
- Derive schedules from participants
- Load scheduling parameters
- Calculate buffer slots
- Use transaction bundle with conditional creates

**Key differences from $hold:**
- Create slots with `status=busy` (not `busy-tentative`)
- Create appointment with `status=booked` (not `pending`)
- No hold expiration tracking needed

## Response Format

### Success

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Bundle",
  "type": "searchset",
  "entry": [{
    "resource": {
      "resourceType": "Appointment",
      "id": "appointment-456",
      "status": "booked",
      "start": "2025-10-28T09:00:00-07:00",
      "end": "2025-10-28T09:30:00-07:00",
      "participant": [...],
      "slot": [{"reference": "Slot/slot-123"}]
    }
  }]
}
```

### Rejection

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Bundle",
  "type": "searchset",
  "entry": [
    {"resource": {"resourceType": "Appointment", "status": "cancelled"}},
    {"resource": {
      "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
      "issue": [{
        "severity": "error",
        "code": "conflict",
        "diagnostics": "Requested time slot is no longer available"
      }]
    }}
  ]
}
```

## Edge Cases

2. **Concurrent bookings**: First succeeds, second conflicts
3. **Invalid status**: Error if not `pending` or `proposed`
4. **Missing slots**: Error for pending without slot references
5. **Slot status mismatch**: Error if not tentative when expected
6. **Booking limit violation**: Return error
7. **Past time**: Return error

## Examples

### Book from Hold

```http
POST [base]/Appointment/$book

{
  "resourceType": "Parameters",
  "parameter": [{
    "name": "appointment",
    "resource": {
      "resourceType": "Appointment",
      "id": "held-123",
      "status": "pending",
      "start": "2025-10-28T09:00:00-07:00",
      "end": "2025-10-28T09:30:00-07:00",
      "participant": [
        {"actor": {"reference": "Practitioner/dr-johnson"}},
        {"actor": {"reference": "Patient/patient-123"}}
      ],
      "slot": [{"reference": "Slot/tentative-1"}]
    }
  }]
}
```

### Direct Book from Find

```http
POST [base]/Appointment/$book

{
  "resourceType": "Parameters",
  "parameter": [{
    "name": "appointment",
    "resource": {
      "resourceType": "Appointment",
      "status": "proposed",
      "start": "2025-10-28T10:00:00-07:00",
      "end": "2025-10-28T10:30:00-07:00",
      "participant": [
        {"actor": {"reference": "Practitioner/dr-johnson"}},
        {"actor": {"reference": "Patient/patient-456"}}
      ]
    }
  }]
}
```

## Testing Scenarios

1. Convert hold to booking (pending → booked)
2. Direct booking without hold
3. Multi-schedule booking via slots
4. Concurrent booking conflicts
5. Expired hold rejection
6. Invalid status rejection
7. Booking limit violation
8. Buffer slot creation
9. Atomic rollback on partial conflicts

By default when enhanced mode (fast ACK) is enabled, we always send both commit ACK and later the application-level ACK (AA, AE, AR).

This corresponds to MSH-16 behavior of `AL` from [Table 0155](https://hl7-definition.caristix.com/v2/HL7v2.7/Tables/0155).

We should allow the endpoint to be configured to any value from the table, especially `ER` (error only) or `NE` (never) modes, which are probably most useful for most configurations.

And then we should update the [appropriate docs](https://www.medplum.com/docs/agent/acknowledgement-modes) to reference the new `Agent Features` entry for this configuration.

medplum/medplum#7785.body:
When using [`enhanced` ACK mode](https://www.medplum.com/docs/agent/acknowledgement-modes#enhanced-acknowledgement-mode-fast-ack), which is part of the HL7v2 spec, it's possible in flows that push messages to other remote systems on receipt of an inbound HL7 message ( **Remote System 1** -> **Agent 1** -> **Bot** -> **Agent 2** -> **Remote System 2** ) that messages at the final destination can arrive out of order, especially when there are variable processing times within bots. 

This can make it hard to process messages in order since one logical inbound channel from remote system 1 can produce messages that arrive out of order from the perspective of **Remote System 2**, due to variance in network connectivity, processing speed of the **Bot** (earlier bot executions can finish after subsequent executions).

However, there is a mechanism in place in the HL7v2 spec which allows for explicit serial processing via a sequence number in `MSH.13`. See: https://hl7-definition.caristix.com/v2/HL7v2.2/Fields/MSH.13

This is a two part solution:
1. **[THIS ISSUE]** - Make it so that a channel can automatically add (or replace) an incoming sequence number on a received message in the order that they are received on the channel. This means that even with the `enhanced` ACK mode turned on, messages will still be numerically sequenced in order from the beginning. This is not the default so if sequence numbers are already being provided by **Remote System 1**, this is not necessary as long as **Agent 2** is configured properly.
2.  #7784 - Make is so that an outbound client (for `pushToAgent` functionality) honors a `Device`'s configuration for `serializedMode` and waits to send a message when a message with a sequence number higher than the next expected sequence number is received. This makes sure that `Remote System 2` will always receive the messages in the order they were received from `Remote System 1` and hence will be processed by the system serially.
medplum/medplum#7785.title: [Agent] Add `serializedMode` to channel endpoints for end-to-end serialized processing

      at sn.createRelatedPersons (/app/node_modules/@medplum/ccda/dist/cjs/index.cjs:2:1312)

medplum/medplum#7864.body:
### Summary

When a Medplum bot uses a npm package that has a transitive dependency on the `TextDecoder` API (such as recent versions of `@google-cloud/vertexai`), the bot fails to execute on the Medplum server. The `AuditEvent` for the execution shows the error `Error: TextDecoder is not defined`.

This issue does not occur in a standard local Node.js environment (e.g., v24), where `TextDecoder` is globally available. This indicates that the Medplum bot execution environment may be a minimal Node.js environment that does not expose this standard API, leading to compatibility issues with a growing number of modern libraries.

### Steps to Reproduce

1.  Create a new Medplum bot.
2.  Add a dependency on a package that relies on `TextDecoder`. For example, a recent version of `@google-cloud/vertexai`.
3.  Bundle the bot and its dependencies into a single file using a bundler like `esbuild`. Ensure the dependency is included in the bundle and not marked as external.
4.  Deploy the bundled bot code to a Medplum project.
5.  Trigger the bot's execution.
6.  Check the `AuditEvent` logs for the project.

### Actual Result

The bot execution fails, and the `AuditEvent` `outcomeDesc` contains the error: `Error: TextDecoder is not defined`.

### Analysis

The root cause appears to be that the V8 Isolate or VM environment used to execute Medplum bots does not provide a global `TextDecoder` or `TextEncoder`, which are standard in modern JavaScript environments (browsers and Node.js).

Several standard polyfill methods fail in this environment:
-   `Object.assign(global, { TextDecoder })`: Fails because `global` is not defined.
-   `esbuild`'s `inject` feature: Fails to correctly apply the polyfill.
-   Importing `text-encoding`: This library also fails to apply the polyfill correctly in the restrictive environment.
medplum/medplum#7864.title: Bot Execution Environment Missing `TextDecoder` vmcontext
